next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 17

Paul watched Tommy through the sliding glass door for a long time before he went out.

Tommy was in the Adirondack chair closest to the railing — Martin's chair, though nobody called it that anymore — with a beer balanced on the armrest he wasn't touching. The propane heater was off. The temperature had dropped into the low fifties after sunset, and Tommy was sitting there in a t-shirt and jeans like he couldn't feel it or didn't care. His hands were on his knees. He was looking at the lake.

Joel was in the study. He'd been there since they read the fire document, doing what Joel did — rereading Martin's careful handwriting for the third time, making notes on his legal pad, building the case against a man he'd known for twenty-five years with the same flat precision he used for people he'd never met. Joel had wanted to go straight to Mallick.

"We hand her this tonight," Joel had said. "The document, the photos on Cliff's phone, the timeline. She's got it."

"I know."

"Then let's go."

"Give me an hour."

Joel looked at him the way he looked at clients who were about to make their own worst decision. "Why?"

Paul didn't have a clean answer. He had the messy kind — because Martin was his best friend and Martin spent twenty-two years protecting Tommy and Paul needed to look at Tommy's face when Tommy heard the words. Because Paul had sat on that deck a hundred times and grilled steaks and argued about the Vikings' secondary and never once understood what was underneath the conversation. Because he'd read Martin's handwriting in the safe and it was the most Mar...
open_hooks_to_carry: Joel and Paul on the deck comparing notes — Paul's suspicion about Martin-Tommy, Joel's observations about Tommy-Cliff
They go to the study and find the fire document in the safe — the envelope has been unfolded twice by different hands
Joel reads Martin's account of the 2002 fire and Ray Toomey's death
The picture assembles: Cliff found the document, Cliff leveraged Tommy, Tommy killed Cliff
Joel and Paul must decide: go to Mallick, confront Tommy, or protect the group one last time the way Martin would have
Joel's legal mind and Paul's loyalty to Martin's memory should pull in opposite directions

payoffs_to_land.description: Joel and Paul find and read the fire document that Cliff discovered in Ch 8 and used as leverage in Ch 12. The crease pattern reveals it was handled by multiple people. The full picture of the murder motive is now assembled.

prev_tail_excerpt:
...BCA team would be here within hours. She'd need to walk the stone path again in daylight, process Cliff's room for anything he'd left behind, and get into that safe.

She thought about Saturday morning. Walking up to this front door with a warm dish in her hands, getting the name wrong, asking about the bathroom. She'd liked these men, in the way she liked most people she investigated — they were scared and human and doing a bad job of hiding it, and the bad job was where the information lived. She'd felt almost fond of their collective bumbling. Five amateur cover-ups on top of a death that didn't need covering.

She didn't feel fond now. One of these men had hit Cliff Haywood hard enough to stagger him, put him in three feet of water, and held him there while he drowned. One of these men had stood in the dark on the dock and felt another person stop breathing under his hands and then walked back up the stone path to the house and gone to bed.

She picked up Nate's phone in its evidence bag. She'd start with the audio. The recordings a man had made for his small, precarious podcast — draft night banter, fantasy football arguments, the ambient sound of an evening among friends — were now the backbone of a murder timeline. The podcaster who couldn't stop recording had captured the sounds of a house where someone was about to die, and he'd done it for content.

She put on her reading glasses and opened the evidence bag.

The refrigerator clicked back on. The loon called once more from the lake, farther away now, barely audible through the kitchen window. Mallick pressed play.
secondary_character_beats: Joel's public defender instincts should make him aware of what this evidence means legally — and what it doesn't prove without confession
state_deltas_required: Joel and Paul assembling the full picture from civilian evidence
Joel and Paul know Tommy killed Cliff — the question is what they do with it
